mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity pricing through a lower discount rate and improved risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is declining month-over-month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recent local low signals weakening labor demand and rising recession odds, which compresses expected earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from 50% at p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient nominal demand and supports near-term revenue expectations, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS surprises are not followed by above-median 5-day SPX returns, or if the relationship flips sign in a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO falls while CPIAUCSL continues to rise, the combination signals supply stress and margin pressure rather than pure demand strength, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines over 2 months while CPIAUCSL rises over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered periods show non-negative average 20-day SPX returns across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST reflects stronger housing demand and broader cyclical momentum, which supports equity returns over the next 60 trading days through improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST is not followed by positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in more than one-third of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS expands while UNRATE falls, the labor market is strengthening in a non-inflationary way that supports earnings and should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 1 month and UNRATE falls over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition fails to predict positive 20-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS combined with accelerating CPIAUCSL signals a policy mistake risk that raises real rates and compresses valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month and CPIAUCSL inflation rate is accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-plus-inflation episodes do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate is not significantly below 50% at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 confirms broad output contraction and lowers expected corporate sales, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days when the contraction persists across consecutive releases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarterly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive quarterly GDPC1 contractions are not followed by negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.
